subject: Biology
title: Molecular Clocks
description: A fast breakdown of Molecular Clocks for builders.

hook.text: DNA mutates at a roughly steady rate. Scientists use this ticking to date when species diverged.

buildup.text: By counting DNA differences between two species, researchers estimate how long ago they shared an ancestor.

discovery.text: Molecular clocks dated the human-chimp split to about 6–7 million years ago—matching fossil evidence.

twist.text: Different genes mutate at different rates. Choosing the wrong clock gives wildly wrong dates.

climax.text: Molecular clocks have rewritten evolutionary timelines for groups with poor fossil records.

punchline.text: DNA is a clock that never stops ticking.

quiz.question: How do molecular clocks estimate when species diverged?
options: By counting DNA mutation differences and their steady rate
